quoted_tweet.text:
The biggest shift in AI isn’t generation anymore.
It’s understanding.

We’re moving past prompts and into something much bigger:
intent → reasoning → creation.

@LumaLabsAI  Uni-1 is now an API.

Which means it’s no longer just a model creators use —
it’s a creative engine developers can build directly into products.

With one API call, teams can bring:

• intent-driven image generation
• consistent characters and visual identity
• natural-language editing

…into apps, workflows, and production systems.

This changes what creative software can feel like.

Instead of forcing users to learn prompts,
products can now understand direction naturally.

That’s the real shift.

AI stops being just a tool that produces outputs…
and starts becoming part of the product experience.

tweets.text:
@straw1choco Totally fair — most AI outputs sound “AI-ish” because people don’t edit them. I treat Claude like a rough draft: rewrite in my own tone, add specific examples, and cut anything generic.

For outreach, it’s powerful if you use it to research + personalize (not spam). Think: 10 high-quality, tailored emails > 100 copy-paste ones.
